releases.notes: Screensaver upload now auto-previews. After the upload finishes the companion app pushes the device into its sleep/screensaver screen via a new BLE command (0x20 GOTO_SLEEP), so you immediately see the image you just uploaded instead of waiting for the 2-minute idle timeout or long-pressing the device. Touch the knob to wake back to MAIN. Also: screensaver upload now propagates a 'commit' error to the app if the file made it to flash but couldn't reload into PSRAM — previously this case silently reported success but left the device with no visible image until reboot.

Touch-lock indicator now silent at rest. MAIN and the gear-pattern screen no longer show a persistent padlock badge — the locked state stays invisible until you actually touch the screen, at which point a 'LOCKED · hold to unlock' toast flashes for 1.5s telling you how to recover. Keeps the gear screens clean while driving but doesn't leave a tester wondering why a tap did nothing.

Stationary IMU jitter fix. When the knob is sitting still (no chassis motion detected), roll/pitch now snap to zero through a 0.6° dead-band — accel micro-noise that used to leak through as a constant wiggle of the G-meter dot is filtered out completely. Heavy 0.04 EMA still catches slow real drift (mounting settling, temperature) so a genuinely-tilted knob does eventually re-zero on its own. Snappy response while actually moving the knob is unchanged.

OTA reboot delay + auto-reconnect. After a firmware update finishes streaming, the device now waits 5 seconds before rebooting and shows 'OTA OK · rebooting in 5s' on screen — gives you visual confirmation the flash actually landed and time to put the knob down before the LCD goes dark. The companion app shows a matching countdown, then automatically tries to reconnect to the same device for up to 30 seconds. When the device comes back, it pairs itself and drops you onto the Live screen without you tapping anything. Same flow on the local-upload path (UPLOAD .BIN) too.

SET ZERO menu row + G-meter preview pitch fix. (1) New 'SET ZERO' option right under CALIBRATE in the main menu — long-press to snap the IMU origin to whatever pose the knob is in right now, without going through the full calibration wizard. Useful when the knob has shifted in its mount but the gear targets are still good. (2) DevicePreview G-meter now also inverts the pitch axis (front/back) to match the device's GMETER_FLIP_Y=true convention — v2.5.29 only flipped roll, so the dot was still landing in the wrong forward/back quadrant when the knob was stationary on a slope or being pitched intentionally.

Touch lock + screensaver gyro-wake + BOOT menu entry. (1) Tap the screen three times on MAIN or PATTERN to LOCK touch — every subsequent gesture is suppressed except a long-press, which unlocks. Useful for resting a finger on the knob without bouncing gears or popping the menu. Small padlock badge shows on locked screens. (2) New 'Wake screensaver on motion' toggle under Custom → Animation & feel. When on, wiggling the knob exits the screensaver back to whichever gear screen you were on (MAIN or PATTERN), so you don't have to tap. Off by default — vibration on rough roads can cross the wake threshold. (3) BOOT row added to the main menu (between INFO and BACK) — long-press to soft-reboot the device without unplugging.

Touch sensitivity overhaul. (1) Dropped the 2-of-2 I2C debounce — concurrent BLE writes + display SPI bursts were making the two reads disagree, dropping the touch entirely. Single-read poll is sufficient since the chip's FingerNum==1 check already rejects bus noise. (2) Active-area gate widened to the full 240×240 panel (was 220×220 inner box). Centre-of-screen touches that landed when the I2C bus was contended were being treated as edge-grip false touches and rejected. (3) readXY() at touch-down retries up to 3× — if the initial coordinate read failed, the swipe baseline used to get re-seeded mid-drag, producing the 'must swipe to the screen edge' complaint. (4) Swipe distance threshold dropped 6 → 4 px for snappier centre-launched swipes.

Calibrate page now lets you switch between PRND / SEQ / HPATTERN and pick gear count (4/5/6) for HPAT + SEQ — no more on-device wizard scrolling. JSON over BLE was getting truncated at NimBLE's 512-byte ATT cap, so config + calibration shapes are trimmed and the PWA fills in static metadata client-side. Screensaver upload chunks now go via WRITE (with ATT response) — fixes the silent packet drops that triggered XE_SIZE after upload. BleAxis transfer-buf char now declares WRITE | WRITE_NR so iOS can pick the reliable path. WebCodecs VideoDecoder fallback added for 4K HEVC clips iPhone Photos exports — pipes through MP4Box + hardware decode. Custom page preview now mirrors the device's live gear + tilt over BLE telemetry.

BLE-first release. All daily features (config, calibration, custom name + colours, telemetry) now go over BLE — no more Wi-Fi AP join. New BLE chunked-transfer protocol carries screensaver uploads (~12 s) and OTA firmware (~3 min) so Wi-Fi is no longer needed for ANY feature. SETUP MODE screen rewritten — single 'PAIR' page, no SoftAP / QR / HTTP server (fixes the SETUP-MODE-causes-reboot crash). G-meter axes inverted to match race convention (left-turn pulls dot right). Companion app: Connect screen is BLE-only, Wi-Fi entry removed. ⚠ ONE-TIME USB FLASH — your current firmware doesn't have the new BLE chunked-transfer chars, so this jump can't OTA. After this install, all future updates flow over BLE OTA.

Pre-release — companion now shows release notes again. Touch active area limited to inner 220×220 (no more edge-grip misfires). WiFi reliability fixes (no power-save, max TX) — OTA + screensaver upload should stay connected on iPhone. Gear shift delay minimum raised to 120 ms (fixes the bounce-back-and-forth bug). G-meter gets a red gyro-rate dot with a ghost trail. Boot now waits for the knob to be still before zero-cal. New: companion is also shipping as a native iOS app via Capacitor.

AXIS V1.0.0 — Public release. SYS panel is now wired up (live device stats, RESTART, FACTORY RESET). Companion-app copy reworked to be customer-facing only: no Arduino IDE / BOOT-button / Sketch references anywhere. Firmware label rebranded from dev tags to 'AXIS V1.0.0' on the device INFO screen and across the API.

v1.2.4 — Re-embed PWA into firmware. The device's bundled PWA hadn't been re-baked since v1.1.x, so opening the companion at 192.168.4.1 (or via captive portal on an AXIS-XXXX-only connection) showed the old single-Accent Brand panel even after the firmware itself was upgraded. Re-ran npm run build:device + embed so the on-device PWA matches the github.io one (per-element colour slots, frame scrubber, transition + gear-anim dropdowns).

v1.2.3 — Two upgrade-path fixes. (1) Gear / meter / name / fg / muted / warn render as solid BLACK after upgrading from v1.2.1 → v1.2.2. Cause: v1.2.1 stored 0 in NVS to mean 'inherit'; v1.2.2 took 0 literally. Fix: on load, treat 0 the same as 'key missing' and use the slot's seeded default. (2) PWA Brand panel still shows only the old single Accent picker. Cause: service worker cached the v1.2.0 bundle and won't activate the new one until the standalone app is fully killed. Fix: skipWaiting + clientsClaim so the new SW takes over on the next page load.

v1.2.2 — Colour slots fully independent + setup-exit shortcut. (1) Every per-element slot (gear, meter, name, fg, muted, warn) is now its own pickable colour with no Linked/inherit toggle — changing Accent doesn't cascade. Sensible defaults seed each slot on first boot. (2) 5-tap on MAIN while in SETUP MODE persists tunables + branding to NVS and tears down the SoftAP, so you can step away from the PWA without rebooting (5-tap outside setup mode still opens the menu).

v1.2.1 — Every text on the device is now themeable. Three new semantic-role colour slots: fg (titles + body text — "APP", "MENU", "BRIGHTNESS", SSID, version), muted (hints + secondary info — "Safari: 192.168.4.1", footer device name default, "HOLD TO EXIT"), and warn (errors + exit prompts — "HOLD: EXIT", "AP FAILED", "WAIT - STILL..."). Each defaults to its current static colour so behaviour matches v1.2.0 until you pick. Brand panel now has 6 slot rows.

v1.2.0 — Five PWA-driven customisation features. (1) APP screen auto-collapses to MAIN once the PWA actually pairs; MAIN shows a small SETUP MODE tag while the SoftAP is still up. (2) Per-element colours: gear digit, G-meter grid, and brand name footer each get their own colour slot (defaults inherit from accent). (3) Page transitions: pick between Fade-to-black (default), Iris zoom, and Instant in TUNE. (4) Gear digit animations: None / Slide / Fade. (5) Media preview now scrubs through every quantised frame of an animated screensaver before upload.

v1.1.8 — Live setting feedback. When the PWA mutates anything (TUNE slider drags, BRAND colour/name, screensaver upload, WIFI), the knob screen pops a 2.5 s toast banner at the bottom edge so you actually see your edit landing on the device. Toast renders over every screen via a Display::push() overlay hook — no per-screen wiring. brightFull/brightDim now re-push to the backlight immediately after patch (previously you had to wait for an FSM transition for the new value to land).

v1.1.7 — CRITICAL: fix PSRAM init failure on v1.1.6. v1.1.6 was built with PSRAM=opi (Octal), but the ESP32-S3R2 chip on this board has QSPI (Quad) 2MB PSRAM. Boot logged 'PSRAM chip is not connected, or wrong PSRAM line mode' and bailed → screensaver alloc returned null → image never loaded (logo fell through). Rebuilt with PSRAM=enabled. Re-flash via USB; existing screensaver file on flash will load on first boot.

v1.1.6 — Fix swapped/teal colours on uploaded screensavers. LovyanGFX's pushImage(uint16_t*) default treats the input buffer as big-endian RGB565, but the PWA writes little-endian (natural ESP32 byte order) — so red pixels were rendering as teal. setSwapBytes(true) around the screensaver blit makes both formats (raw RGB565 stills + AXSV animations) display correctly. No re-upload needed — your existing screensaver fixes itself after OTA.

v1.1.5 — Image decoder hardening. PWA now uses createImageBitmap() with explicit sRGB colour-space conversion (was HTMLImageElement which iOS Safari fed wide-gamut/HDR pixels for some AVIFs → shifted colours on device). Better fidelity across formats.

v1.1.4 — Free FS space BEFORE accepting screensaver upload. Was leaving the existing 115 KB screensaver in place while writing a 115 KB tmp, overflowing the 128 KB partition users get when v1.1.1 was OTA-installed instead of USB-flashed. Now removes the old file + reports free space first.

v1.1.3 — Screensaver upload tolerance. Disk write now decoupled from PSRAM reload: HTTP 200 returned as soon as the file is on flash, even if the in-memory reload fails (image still shows on next reboot). Serial.printf at every failure point so 400s become diagnosable. Response now includes frames/fps/animated.

v1.1.2 — Fix funky static-image colours. Stills now go through the legacy raw RGB565 path (full 16-bit colour, no palette quantization). Animations still use AXSV/16-colour (necessary to fit ~20 frames in FS). PWA preview shows decoded result before upload.

v1.1.1 — Animation screensaver (AXSV format). Custom partition: 640 KB LittleFS (was 128 KB), 1664 KB app slots. Upload video/GIF/image via BRAND → Screensaver; PWA quantises to 16 colours + 4-bit indexed format. Up to ~22 frames @ 240×240, configurable FPS. ⚠ ONE-TIME USB FLASH — partition table change cannot OTA. After this install, OTA continues normally.

v1.1.0 — Station-mode OTA. Configure home WiFi in BRAND tab, device joins AP+STA. OTA RELEASES → INSTALL then makes the DEVICE download the .bin via its own WiFi (HTTPClient/TLS), bypassing iOS Safari's cross-origin binary fetch issues entirely.

v1.0.6 — Kill DNS hijack entirely. Was breaking OTA fetches by caching the spoofed reply on iOS. Lose: iOS captive-portal auto-popup. Gain: every OTA fetch now reliably reaches github.io. Open Safari manually → 192.168.4.1 (URL shown on APP screen).

v1.0.5 — OTA fallback: each release has a DOWNLOAD link (Safari native) alongside INSTALL. iOS Safari silently blocks XHR cross-origin binary fetches from HTTP origin; the link bypasses that, save to Files, UPLOAD tab to flash. Also includes v1.0.4 Main screen polish.
v1.0.4 — Polish: Main screen mode label now uses your brand accent colour; device name shown at the bottom. First release intended for OTA install via RELEASES tab end-to-end.
v1.0.3 — DNS hijack TTL 60s → 1s. Was caching bogus reply on iOS, blocking github.io fetch for ~1 min after hijack window closed. Also added 'Safari: 192.168.4.1' hint on APP screen.
v1.0.2 — fix OTA download (Safari ReadableStream → XHR arraybuffer).
v1.0.1 — DNS hijack auto-stops after 30s (was preventing OTA from fetching github.io). USB flash this once, then future updates via OTA.
v1.0.0 — Stable baseline. P1 pairing + captive portal · P2 tune · P3 calibrate · P4 telemetry · P5 brand · P6 screensaver. Digit-only WiFi pwd. iOS: open in Safari for OTA (not Captive Wi-Fi popup).
